MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FILED BY DEFENDANT CHRIST UNITED PRESBYTERIAN CHURCH

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT CHRIST UNITED PRESBYTERIAN CHURCH NOTICE OF MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS OF JOSE VILCHEZ AND FOR AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED, FOR REASONS SET FORTH IN THE MOVING PAPERS AND THE MOTION IS UNOPPOSED. THIRD PARTY JOSE VILCHEZ IS ORDERED TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION TO ANSWER QUESTIONS AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS AS REQUESTED, WITHIN 14 DAYS OF THE HEARING ON THIS MOTION, OR BY OTHER TIME TO BE AGREE BY COUNSEL AND CONFIRMED IN WRITING. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST MR. VILCHEZ IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $350.00. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: KATHERINE HUIBONHOA, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
ARBITRATOR JURISDICTION CALENDAR LIST
Off Calendar Apr-04-2014 Arbitration Award Filed

Ntc Of Mtn To Compel And Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Davis J. Reilly, Esq. of Bledsoe Cathcart Diestel Pedersen & Treppa LLP, Tel. 415.981.5411, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: Prior to the hearing, defendant's counsel submitted a stipulated order compelling plaintiff's independent medical examination & for sanctions. The party stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge. DEFENDANT CHRIST UNITED PRESBYTERIAN CHURCH'S Motion To Compel An Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff And For Sanctions is ordered off calendar as parties have reached an agreement. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn To Compel The Deposition And Production Of Documents Of Jose Vilchez And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Davis J. Reilly, Bledsoe Cathcart Deistel Pedersen & Treppa, 415.981.5411, appearing for the Defendant Christ United Presbyterian Church;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

The Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CHRIST UNITED PRESBYTERIAN CHURCH Notice Of Motion To Compel The Deposition And Production Of Documents Of Jose Vilchez And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions is Granted, for reasons set forth in the moving papers and the motion is unopposed. Third party Jose Vilchez is ordered to appear for deposition to answer questions and produce documents as requested, within 14 days of the hearing on this motion, or by other time to be agree by counsel and confirmed in writing. Defendant's request for sanctions against Mr. Vilchez is granted in the amount of $350.00. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Huibonhoa, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not reported. =(302/JPT)
